title: Infer latent cheap-stop regimes from synchronized field anomalies
summary: FIA VSC/SC rules force all cars above a minimum delta, and official F1 analysis notes Safety Car conditions can roughly halve pit-loss.
rationale: FIA VSC/SC rules force all cars above a minimum delta, and official F1 analysis notes Safety Car conditions can roughly halve pit-loss. Those regime shifts radically change stop incentives but are not explicit in the dataset. A latent detector built from field-wide same-lap anomalies can approximate hidden neutralization state.
implementation_hint: For each `(Year,Race,LapNumber)`, build same-lap aggregates such as field median lap-time delta, spread compression, unusually high pit-count on the lap, and abrupt cross-driver slowdown. Use these to create a neutralization probability or regime flag, then gate a specialized expert or interact it with pit-pressure features.
expected_effect: Should sharpen predictions for clustered pit laps and explain cases where teams stop earlier than tyre-life heuristics alone would suggest.
risk: The proxy can confuse weather, red-flag aftermath, or testing sessions with SC/VSC behavior; overfitting is likely if the detector is too hand-tuned.
